source_jobs.title: In-Orbit Services Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.



Our Future Systems unit is dedicated to expanding and complementing these capabilities by conceptualizing and pre-developing new technologies, products and programs.



We are seeking a talented Mechanical Engineer to help us in our mission by contributing to the development of our future projects. As a Mechanical Engineer, you will be in charge of the mechanical sizing and analysis of a variety of new projects, especially within the In-Orbit Services team. You will work closely with our thermal, CAD, and system engineers to assess feasibility and preliminary sizing of mechanical systems. You will be part of the In-Orbit Services Team within the Future Systems Department, and will report to the Principal Engineer of the project and the Future Systems Lead.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

In your capacity as a Mechanical Engineer, your role will be continuously evolving, but day-to-day your duties will include:

 - Contributing to co-engineering sessions with CAD, thermal and system engineers

 - Determining the mechanical load cases throughout the entire project lifecycle

 - Providing technical solutions to optimize mechanical performance, in collaboration with other subsystems (propulsion, avionics…)

 - Contributing to the creation and improvement of mechanical analysis tools

 - Effectively communicating and providing recommendations through analyses, preparation, and presentation of technical data

 - Preparing technical documentation and participating in design reviews




WHAT WE WOULD LOVE TO SEE FROM YOU

In the role of In-Orbit Services Mechanical Engineer, ideally, you will have the following:

 - Education: Master’s or PhD degree in Aerospace Engineering, Mechanical Engineering or a related field.

 - Technical skills: Strong knowledge in mechanical analysis, coupled with sound design skills

 - Experience: Hands-on experience in preliminary development phase (Phase B/C) project work and systems engineering

 - Programming Skills: Proficiency in programming and data analysis (e.g., Python, Altair Hypermesh, MSC NASTRAN).

 - Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

 - Feedback: Willingness to give proactive and mindful feedback across all company levels.

 - Mindset: Passion for advancing sustainability in the space industry and highly motivated to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding start-up environment.

 - Languages: Fluent in English.



Why you should join us!

What makes us special here at The Exploration Company and why we think you will enjoy working here is:  

 - We’re Agile - we make decisions fast whilst keeping our goals and systems in mind 

 - We’re Open and Collaborative - we are transparent about risks and obstacles, so that we can cooperate to overcome them  

 - We have a lot of Fun - we refuel our energy knowing we are democratising space. It’s a wonderful and rare opportunity, are YOU up for the challenge?  

We'd love to hear from you if you wish to be a part of our journey. Please submit your CV now for immediate consideration and we will be in touch shortly.

source_jobs.title: Lunar Lander Test Bench Development Senior Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.



At The Exploration Company, we are developing next-generation space transportation systems, including a lunar lander capable of precision landing and reusable operations. To support this effort, we are establishing a dedicated remote test site in the United Arab Emirates to perform hot-fire propulsion testing and hopper flight demonstrations.



As a Lunar Lander Test Bench Development Engineer, you will lead the design, development, and deployment of this new test facility from concept to operations. The role combines propulsion test infrastructure design, cryogenic fluid systems engineering, civil infrastructure coordination, and end-to-end project execution in a remote environment.



This position requires periodic travel to the UAE during site development, commissioning, and operations phases.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES / TASKS

In this role, your responsibilities will evolve with project maturity. Day-to-day duties will include:



Test Site Development

 - Define and synthesize system-level requirements for a remote lunar lander test site supporting hot firing and hopper flight testing.

 - Lead the design and implementation of the complete facility, including ground infrastructure and utilities.

 - Specify and implement propellant and pressurization systems including Liquid Oxygen, Liquid Methane, Helium, and Nitrogen.

 - Design infrastructure enabling autonomous operation of a remote site (power generation, storage, logistics, safety systems).



Fluid and Cryogenic Systems Engineering

 - Develop P&IDs for propulsion test facilities and associated ground support equipment.

 - Design, size, and select components for cryogenic and high-pressure fluid systems.

 - Ensure safe handling, storage, and distribution of cryogenic propellants and pressurants.



Infrastructure and Civil Engineering Coordination

 - Define facility layout, foundations, structural interfaces, and site infrastructure requirements.

 - Coordinate with civil engineering partners to implement test stands, safety perimeters, and support buildings.

Safety, Automation, and Operations

 - Define and implement automation architectures for test operations and facility control, including safe connections with the vehicle to test.

 - Develop and validate safety-critical systems protecting personnel, hardware, and environment.

 - Perform hazard analyses (FMEA, HAZOP, risk assessments) and implement mitigation measures.

 - Develop test procedures, operational concepts, and commissioning plans.



Project Leadership

 - Lead end-to-end development of the test facility including design, procurement, manufacturing, installation, commissioning, and operations readiness.

 - Manage technical interfaces with internal teams, customers, and external suppliers.

 - Perform trade-off analyses to optimize performance, safety, cost, and schedule.

 - Support on-site deployment, integration, and test campaigns in the UAE.

 


WHAT WE WOULD LOVE TO SEE FROM YOU

 - Degree in aerospace engineering, mechanical engineering, energy systems, or a related field.

 - 5+ years of experience in propulsion test facilities or hot-fire test bench development.

 - Strong expertise in fluid systems engineering, including cryogenic propellants.

 - Experience designing or operating ground support equipment for rocket propulsion systems.

 - Knowledge of automation systems and safety-critical control architectures for industrial or test environments.

 - Experience coordinating infrastructure or facility development in industrial or remote environments.

 - Demonstrated project management capability across multidisciplinary engineering activities.

 - Hands-on engineering mindset with strong problem-solving ability.

source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - System Architecture & Design
   
   - Define the Electrical Power System architecture in coordination with system engineering and mission requirements.
   
   - Size and specify solar arrays, batteries, and power conditioning/distribution units.
   
   - Ensure optimal power generation, storage, and distribution strategy across all mission phases and failure scenario and failure scenario

 - Component Selection & Specification
   
   - Evaluate and select flight-qualified components: solar cells, battery design, power conversion and protection elements.
   
   - Support the design of power conditioning and distribution units
   
   - Generate technical specifications

 - Analysis & Simulation
   
   - Conduct worst-case, reliability, and redundancy analyses in accordance with ECSS/NASA standards.

 - Integration & Testing
   
   - Define and execute Electrical Power System verification and validation plans (unit, subsystem, and system levels).
   
   - Support integration of Electrical Power System with other subsystems of the spacecraft.
   
   - Participate in environmental testing: vibration, thermal vacuum, EMC.

 - Project & Supplier Coordination
   
   - Interface with suppliers for COTS and internal teams for in-house designed power system components.
   
   - Support project development logic, planning, risk assessment, and technical reviews (PDR, CDR, TRR).

 - Documentation & Standards Compliance
   
   - Produce detailed documentation for design, analysis, test procedures, and compliance reports.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or related field.

 - Minimum 3–5 years of experience in spacecraft Electrical Power System or avionics system development.

 - Good level of understanding of the following domains:
   
   - Solar array systems
   
   - Space-qualified batteries
   
   - Power converters (DC/DC), MPPT, PCDU design
   
   - Power distribution architectures (bus, switched, redundant)
   
   - General electronics

 - Proficiency with simulation and software development tools (e.g. Github, Python).

 - Familiarity with ECSS/NASA standards and derating rules for space hardware.

 - Strong analytical, problem-solving, and technical documentation skills.

source_jobs.title: Senior Additive Manufacturing Materials Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.


In addition to Nyx, we are actively developing Typhoon, the largest full-flow staged combustion engine ever to be built in Europe.

We are looking for a talented Senior Additive Manufacturing Materials Engineer to join our mission and contribute significantly to the development and in-house production of additive manufacturing (AM) technologies for our propulsion products powering all our space vehicles.



ROLE OVERVIEW

The ideal candidate will play a key role in the qualification and development of AM processes, including the definition and characterization of print parameters for new materials on larger, more productive machines. This role also involves supporting the certification of AM components to meet high industry standards, including NASA regulations. You will help implement large-scale Directed Energy Deposition (DED) processes and develop functionally graded or bi-metallic components. This is a versatile position with broad hands-on responsibilities in additive manufacturing reporting to TEC's Lead for AM.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and execute materials development and maturation plans for new processes using established alloys, and for existing processes with new alloys.

 - Serve as a Materials Engineer for AM projects, collaborating with cross-functional teams to support the development and production of innovative components and systems.

 - Operate AM machines in a development environment as needed.

 - Assist in the development of new alloys for AM, including maturation of atomization methods and creation of powder specifications.

 - Support the qualification and testing of AM processes to ensure compliance with industry standards and performance requirements.

 - Responsible for onsite materials and powder characterization equipment & laboratory, including selection of equipment, budget for consumables and maintenance, training and standard operating procedures.

 - Collaborate on the certification of AM processes and parts, particularly those required to meet NASA and aerospace standards.

 - Develop post-process heat treatment procedures and specifications.

 - Document results and contribute to technical reports and procedures related to AM process qualification and development.

 - Troubleshoot and improve AM processes for enhanced performance and reliability.

 - Stay up to date with the latest advancements in AM technologies and materials.

 - Act as an onsite quality organization delegate.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Engineering degree in Materials Science , Metallurgy, Welding or a related field.

 - Experience with AM processes such as laser powder bed fusion and directed energy deposition.

 - Strong knowledge of material properties, behavior, testing, and characterization—especially metals used in aerospace propulsion applications.

 - Familiarity with additive manufacturing standards, including NASA or aerospace industry standards, is highly desirable.

 - Experience working in an ISO 9001 or similar manufacturing environment producing and delivering hardware.

 - Extreme sense of ownership and responsibility for work assigned, striving to complete on-time , exceptional quality, and under budget.

 - Strong analytical skills and a hands-on approach to problem-solving.

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in both internal and external teams.

 - Ability to manage multiple tasks and adapt to a dynamic work environment.



PREFERRED SKILLS

 - Experience with materials modeling, including microstructure and property prediction.

 - Proficiency in CAD and simulation software (e.g., NX Suite, ANSYS).

 - Familiarity with process qualification and statistical analysis tools.

 - Knowledge of quality control methodologies for AM

source_jobs.title: In-Orbit Services Fault Management Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.



Our In-Orbit Services unit is dedicated to expanding and complementing these capabilities by conceptualizing and developing new technologies, products and programs.



We are seeking a methodical Fault Management Engineer to help us strike the right balance between performance and reliability. The In-Orbit Services Fault Management Engineer is responsible for ensuring the reliability, availability, maintainability, and safety of orbital service vehicles and systems. This role is central to the design, development, and operational assurance of in-orbit service missions, supporting both technical risk reduction and mission success.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



 - Fault Management Strategy:
   
   - Define, implement and validate fault management strategies, including Failure Detection, Isolation, and Recovery (FDIR) logic for in-orbit service vehicles.
   
   - Coordinate between hardware and flight software teams to architect robust and lean fault management solutions.



 - RAMS Analyses & Assurance:
   
   - Lead the definition and execution of fault tree analyses (FTA) and Failure Modes, Effects, and Criticality Analyses (FMECA) to identify and mitigate potential failure modes.
   
   - Perform reliability and availability prediction studies and allocate reliability targets to subsystems and equipment.
   
   - Conduct hazard analyses for ground, orbital, and re-entry phases, ensuring compliance with relevant safety requirements, including the safety analysis for launch authorities.



 - Continuous Improvement:
   
   - Collect and analyze in-orbit data to inform lessons learned and drive improvements in fault management and overall system reliability.
   
   - Support the creation and improvement of RAMS analysis tools and technical documentation.



 - Collaboration & Communication:
   
   - Work closely with multidisciplinary engineering teams (mechanical, thermal, avionics, software, operations, systems) to integrate RAMS and fault management requirements throughout the project lifecycle.
   
   - Prepare and present technical data, recommendations, and reports to internal and external stakeholders.




WHAT WE WOULD LOVE TO SEE FROM YOU

source_jobs.title: Wire Harness Technician (EWIS)
source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.



To support this mission, we are bringing harness manufacturing in-house and are looking for a Wire Harness Technician to help industrialize, build, and validate high-reliability electrical harnesses used across the Nyx avionics and test campaigns.

In this role, your scope will be continuously evolving. Day-to-day you will help translate harness design outputs into robust manufacturing processes and deliver flight-like and test harnesses with strong quality, repeatability, and documentation.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

In your capacity as a Wire Harness Technician, your responsibilities will typically include:



Manufacturing & Assembly Execution



 - Build electrical harnesses (development, qualification, and test harnesses), including cut/strip, crimp, splice, solder (where applicable), lacing/tie, over-braid/shield terminations, labeling, and final assembly.

 - Support batch-by-batch production for test campaigns and verification activities (e.g., shock/vibration, EMI/EMC, TVAC), coordinating readiness of parts, tooling, and work instructions.



Cross-functional Collaboration



 - Work closely with harness design and integration stakeholders to ensure manufacturability (DFM), clarity of drawings, and alignment between wiring diagrams and physical assembly.



Documentation & Traceability



 - Maintain “as-built” records, deviations, rework logs, and configuration/serial tracking for harnesses and critical components.




WHAT WE WOULD LOVE TO SEE FROM YOU



For the Wire Harness Technician role, you ideally bring experience with the following:

 - Technical vocational training (Ausbildung) in electrical, electronic, or mechanical fields (e.g. Elektroniker, Mechatroniker) or equivalent hands-on experience.

 - Strong hands-on experience with wiring harness manufacturing or cable assembly in high-reliability contexts (aerospace/space, aviation, motorsport, defense, medical, or similar).

 - Ability to interpret harness drawings, wiring diagrams, BOMs, and connector tables; comfortable working from controlled documentation and detailed work instructions.

 - Solid understanding of harness workmanship principles (crimp quality, strain relief, routing/bend radius, shielding/grounding practices, backshell assembly and torqueing, etc.).

 - Practical troubleshooting skills (identifying pinning errors, shorts/opens, intermittent faults, damaged contacts, shielding issues, etc.).

 - Experience using typical harness assembly tools (crimp tools, wire strippers, insertion/extraction tools, torque tools, soldering equipment when applicable).

 - High attention to detail and manual dexterity, with a strong focus on quality and workmanship standards (preferably IPC/WHMA awareness).

 - Ability to follow precise processes and work instructions while maintaining consistency and traceability during production.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced production or test environment where priorities can shift based on integration and delivery needs.

 - Team-oriented mindset with clear communication with engineering and quality teams when issues arise.




NICE TO HAVE



 - Familiarity with aerospace/space workmanship standards and inspection approaches (e.g., IPC-style workmanship, connector acceptance criteria, traceability practices).

 - Experience with harness electrical test equipment and automated test report generation.

 - Understanding of EMI/EMC-sensitive build practices (shield termination strategies, pigtail control, bonding/grounding considerations), aligned with the fact that harnesses support EMI/EMC test activities.

 - Prior experience supporting test harness builds for environmental testing (vibration, thermal-vac, etc.).

source_jobs.title: Senior People Partner
source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.



"If you want to build a rocket, don't assign tasks — ignite a longing for the endless immensity of the stars."






THIS IS NOT AN ORDINARY WORKPLACE

 

We build space vehicles for humanity. Not as a tagline. Not as a marketing line. As the literal, daily reality of every person on our team. We are in the middle of a space exploration renaissance, one that happens once in a generation. In just four years, we built two demonstrator capsules, signed a Space Act Agreement with NASA (the first European company ever to do so), booked 10 missions, and began developing Storm, a high-thrust staged combustion rocket engine. We operate across seven countries, backed by tier-one investors. And we are just getting started. If you join TEC, you are not taking a job. You are choosing an expedition.

 

Our mission: We build space vehicles for humanity, enabling everyone to participate peacefully in the building of our human future in space.

 


WHY YOU MIGHT NOT JOIN US AND WHY THE RIGHT PERSON WILL

 

1. We work on-site, together. Minimum three days a week in person, full presence. We'll always work with real life but our baseline is genuine commitment. If you need a fully remote role, we're probably not the right fit right now.

 

2. We are in build season. The pace is high, the stakes are real, the urgency is genuine. Steady and comfortable won't feel like enough here.

 

3. We expect extreme ownership. We want people who feel the mission personally, who care about Nyx, Storm and what we're building beyond their job description. This culture asks a lot. Willingly.

 

But if this excites you more than it worries you, few people get the chance to work on something history will remember. Nobody climbs Everest without full commitment. Nobody builds a space capsule or a rocket engine without it either. This is that moment. This is that team.

 


THE ROLE

 

Reporting to the Chief People Officer, you'll be a trusted advisor to leaders and teams across a global business of 600 people. You'll lead on our most critical people priorities, performance, development, culture, leadership coaching and complex global projects. This is not a support role. It's a seat at the table.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Performance & growth Own and evolve our performance philosophy, build frameworks that inspire growth rather than generate anxiety; use people data to drive smart decisions

 - People development Design and deliver programmes that stretch and retain talent, champion continuous learning from IC to senior leadership

 - Leadership coaching Be a trusted advisor and coach to senior leaders, navigate complex challenges with confidence, discretion and sound judgement

 - Culture & engagement Guard and grow the TEC culture, bring intentionality to the moments that matter

 - Global projects Lead cross-functional people initiatives across geographies; bring structure and pace to ambiguous, high-stakes programmes


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Significant experience as a senior HR Business Partner or People Partner in a fast-scaling, international organisation

 - A track record of leading on performance, development and transformation, not just supporting it

 - The confidence to challenge and advise senior stakeholders with credibility and warmth

 - Comfortable with people data, you use it to tell compelling stories

 - Fluency in English essential, German or French an advantage

 - Curiosity about our mission matters more than aerospace experience, though both is ideal


WHAT WE OFFER

 - High-impact role with real autonomy to shape how people partnering works at TEC

 - Collaborative, international team that values substance over hierarchy

 - Salary: €77,500 – €105,000 per annum, depending on experience

 - Relocat

source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are developing the avionics system of a rocket engine powered by electric pumps. The team is responsible for the design, integration, testing, and qualification of the engine electronics and embedded software.




We are looking for an Senior Electronics Hardware Test & Qualification Engineer to help structure, execute, and industrialize our electronics test activities. This role is intended for someone who is both rigorous in test definition and reporting, and hands-on in the lab.

The objective is to help mature our electronics hardware from prototype bring-up to qualified and deliverable units by defining test strategies, preparing test campaigns, building test means, executing tests, analyzing results, and producing clear verification evidence.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES / TASKS





We are looking for someone who enjoys the interface between engineering design and real hardware testing. The ideal candidate is rigorous enough to define proper test strategies and qualification evidence, but practical enough to build a test adapter, debug a signal on an oscilloscope, and improve a lab setup.

This person should be comfortable working in a fast-moving development environment where hardware is still evolving, tests need to be structured, and technical issues must be solved pragmatically. The role requires both engineering discipline and hands-on ownership.



 - Define, structure, and maintain electronics test strategies for ECU, inverter electronics, and future control boards, from development testing to qualification and acceptance.

 - Translate requirements into test cases, procedures, acceptance criteria, and verification evidence, ensuring clear requirements-to-test traceability.

 - Prepare, organize, and execute electronics test campaigns, including board bring-up, functional testing, hardware/software integration, inverter testing, and power HIL.

 - Design, build, and maintain hands-on test means such as test boards, breakout boards, harnesses, adapters, loads, and instrumentation setups.

 - Analyze test results, write clear test reports, document deviations, and support qualification or delivery reviews.

 - Support hardware debugging and anomaly investigations using schematics, measurements, logs, and lab instrumentation.

 - Improve test bench robustness, repeatability, safety, configuration control, and automation.

 - Work closely with electronics, power electronics, embedded software, control, mechanical/thermal, supplier, and test teams to ensure avionics hardware is testable, integrated, and deliverable.
   
   
   
   
   


WHAT WE WOULD LOVE TO SEE FROM YOU



 - Hands-on experience in electronics hardware testing, board bring-up, debugging, validation, or qualification.

 - Ability to read schematics, PCB documentation, datasheets, interface specifications, and requirements.

 - Experience preparing test plans, test procedures, acceptance criteria, and test reports.

 - Practical experience with lab equipment such as oscilloscopes, power supplies, electronic loads, DAQs, multimeters, logic analyzers, CAN tools, or power analyzers.

source_jobs.title: Development Models Simulations Intern
source_jobs.description:
Here at The Exploration Company, we are developing, producing, and operating Nyx, a modular and reusable space orbital vehicle that can eventually be refuelled in orbit and that can carry cargo - and potentially humans in the longer run.



The heart of a spacecraft lies in its propulsion system, and that's why we, at The Exploration Company, are dedicated to developing our own cutting-edge Propulsion Systems ensuring the highest level of efficiency and reliability in our space endeavors.

We want you to support us in the development of simulation models to predict the behavior of the pressurization and feed system of the propulsion system. The models are crucial for predicting the behavior of test benches and more importantly the flight vehicle behavior. You will be embedded in the simulation team who are also a crucial part of the testing team as they support in data analysis and test predictions

Activities:

 - Anchoring of the development models of the Propulsion System of our Nyx Earth propulsion system

 - Implementing automated post processing scripts of simulation results.

 - Development of automated optimization algorithms to allow for rapid regression of simulation results against experimental test data.

 - Support the post-processing strategy for experimental results for use in validation of simulation models.

 - Act as auxiliary support to test campaigns when needed.

Competencies:

 - Strong understanding of fluid dynamics and the development of fluid flow systems.

 - Understanding of general model anchoring.

 - Understanding of optimization strategies for model anchoring.

 - Competency with data management in python using standard python libraries such as NumPy/Pandas

Desirables:

 - Experience in the development of models in Ecosim.

 - Hands-on experience on development of fluidic test benches.



Why you should join us!

What makes us special here at The Exploration Company and why we think you will enjoy working here is:  

 - We’re Agile - we make decisions fast whilst keeping our goals and systems in mind
